Doghmush clan.description: A Palestinian clan from the Gaza Strip involved in extortion, smuggling, arms dealing, and killings. Known for its long-running rivalry with Hamas and affiliation with various militant groups including Fatah, Hamas, and Al-Qaeda. The clan was behind the 2007 kidnapping of British journalist Alan Johnston. Many members were killed in Israeli airstrikes during the 2023 Gaza war.

descriptions: Clan members are affiliated or aligned with Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the clan are affiliated or aligned with various Palestinian and Islamist groups, including Fatah, Hamas, Popular Resistance Committees, and Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Clan members are affiliated or aligned with Fatah.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the clan are affiliated or aligned with various Palestinian and Islamist groups, including Fatah, Hamas, Popular Resistance Committees, and Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: The clan has a long-running rivalry with Hamas.
edges.evidence_quote: The Doghmush family ... is a Palestinian clan from the Gaza Strip known for its long-running rivalry with Hamas.

descriptions: The clan engaged in violent clashes with Hamas security forces after Hamas took over Gaza.
edges.evidence_quote: After Hamas' takeover of Gaza, the Doghmush clan was often involved in violent and deadly clashes with Hamas security forces.

descriptions: Mumtaz Doghmush, a clan leader, led the Army of Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Mumtaz Doghmush, who was involved in the kidnapping of Gilad Shalit, led the Army of Islam by 2008.

descriptions: Clan members are affiliated or aligned with the Popular Resistance Committees.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the clan are affiliated or aligned with various Palestinian and Islamist groups, including Fatah, Hamas, Popular Resistance Committees, and Al-Qaeda.

Black Mafia.description: African-American organized crime syndicate based in Philadelphia, founded around 1969 by Samuel Christian. At its height in the 1970s-early 1980s, it controlled drug trafficking, extortion, gambling, and other rackets in Philadelphia and the Delaware Valley. The group was known for its use of Islam and intimidation to evade law enforcement. It was responsible for over 40 murders and was largely defunct by 1984.

descriptions: The Gambino Crime Family did business with the growing black organized crime that became the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Bruno , the don of the Philadelphia crime family , discouraged drug dealing in South Philadelphia, but could not prevent deals being made by the New York mafia families such as the Gambino Crime Family , which were doing business with the growing black organized crime that became the Black Mafia

descriptions: The Junior Black Mafia is mentioned as a successor or splinter group of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Junior Black Mafia

descriptions: Black Mafia members created the JBM as a successor organization.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1985, members of the original Black Mafia organized African-American youths into the Junior Black Mafia (JBM)

descriptions: Black Mafia listed Nation of Islam as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council, Nation of Islam, Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: Many Black Mafia members converted to Islam and were associated with the Nation of Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Many of the original members eventually became Nation of Islam members or converted to Islam , giving the organization the nickname of 'the Muslim Mafia' or 'the Muslim Mob.'

descriptions: The infobox lists the Philadelphia crime family as an ally of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council , Nation of Islam , Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: Black Mafia listed Philadelphia crime family as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council, Nation of Islam, Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The infobox lists the Shower Posse as a rival of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Shower Posse , and various other gangs in the Philadelphia area, including their allies

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Shower Posse, and various other gangs in the Philadelphia area, including their allies

descriptions: The infobox lists The Council as an ally of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council , Nation of Islam , Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: Black Mafia listed The Council as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council, Nation of Islam, Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: Black Mafia superseded the weakened Ghetto Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Black Mafia (BM) was one of the gangs that superseded the now weakened and fragmented Ghetto Boys

descriptions: Black Mafia formed to take over illegal activities from the Philadelphia family, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Samuel "Beyah" Christian and other African-American organized crime figures formed the Black Mafia to take control of illegal activities in the Black neighborhoods of Philadelphia from the Italian Mafia, a venture which was partially successful.

descriptions: Listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Mafia

descriptions: Infobox lists Black Mafia as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Mafia, Hells Angels MC, and various other gangs in the Philadelphia area, including their allies

descriptions: The infobox lists the Black Mafia as a rival of the Philadelphia crime family.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Black Mafia as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Mafia

descriptions: After conflict, Philadelphia family allowed Black Mafia to operate some rackets in exchange for a street tax.
edges.evidence_quote: Bruno eventually acquiesced control of some gambling rackets which had historically been dominated by Italian-American mobsters. As per the agreement, Black gangsters were required to pay a "street tax" to the Bruno family in order to engage in the rackets.

descriptions: Infobox lists Castellammarese Mafia clan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Castellammarese Mafia clan

descriptions: The Bonanno family has a strong link with the Castellammarese Mafia clan, with meetings and reference points.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2020, the Italian police together with the FBI discovered a strong link between the Bonannos and the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family, in particular to Francesco Domingo.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Castellammarese Mafia clan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Castellammarese Mafia clan

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Castellammarese Mafia clan

descriptions: The Bonanno family lists the Castellammarese Mafia clan as an ally in the infobox.

descriptions: Infobox lists Castellammarese clan as an ally.

descriptions: Bonanno family lists Castellammarese Mafia clan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Castellammarese Mafia clan

descriptions: Buffalo boss Magaddino and Detroit boss Milazzo supported their Castellammarese allies against Masseria.
edges.evidence_quote: Gaspar Milazzo and his longtime ally, Buffalo Mafia boss Stefano Magaddino had secretly, but willingly supported their allies in New York and Chicago as they opposed Joe Masseria and his supporters.

descriptions: The Castellammare family has close relations with the Bonanno crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: the boss has maintained close relations with the branches of the Cosa Nostra in the United States, in particular with the Bonannos

descriptions: The Castellammarese Mafia and the Buccellato clan were in violent conflict in Brooklyn.
edges.evidence_quote: Continued violence between the two groups caused Milazzo and Magaddino to flee New York City in 1921 after they were sought for questioning in connection with the murder of a Buccellato clan member.

descriptions: Castellammarese clan allied with Buffalo family during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were Buffalo family boss Stefano Magaddino

descriptions: Castellammarese clan had a close relationship with Profaci family.
edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano was also close to Joseph Profaci, future boss of the New York Profaci family.

descriptions: Castellammarese clan allied with Detroit family during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were ... Detroit family boss Gaspar Milazzo

descriptions: The Castellammare family is a constituent part of the mandamento of Alcamo.
edges.evidence_quote: the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family, which in turn belongs to the mandamento of Alcamo

descriptions: Castellammarese clan fought Masseria clan in the Castellammarese War.
edges.evidence_quote: violence broke out between a faction led by Giuseppe "Joe the Boss" Masseria and members of the Castellammareses over the theft of Masseria's bootleg liquor. This soon developed into a full out war known as the Castellammarese War.

descriptions: Castellammarese clan allied with Philadelphia family during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were ... Philadelphia family boss Salvatore Sabella

descriptions: Castellammarese clan secretly allied with Reina family.
edges.evidence_quote: they established a secret alliance with the Bronx Reina family boss Gaetano Reina, a nominal Masseria ally.

descriptions: The Castellammare del Golfo family is a part of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Francesco Domingo (born 1956) is a member of the Sicilian Mafia and the boss of the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family

descriptions: Chicago boss Aiello was an ally of the Castellammarese Mafia against Masseria and Capone.
edges.evidence_quote: By 1930, both men openly supported their allies, including Chicago Mafia boss Giuseppe Aiello, who was being opposed by a powerful Masseria ally in Chicago, Al Capone.

descriptions: The Masseria family and the Castellammarese clan were at war during the Castellammarese War.
edges.evidence_quote: violence erupted between the two rival New York City families. The violence became known as the Castellammarese War, Joe Masseria with his Mafia family battled with Salvatore Maranzano, who was the boss of the Brooklyn-based Castellammarese clan

descriptions: Drive & Marlowe is in alliance with or subsumed into Mali Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Many smaller local gangs are in alliance or have been subsumed into the Mali Boys, such as Drive & Marlowe (DM), Boundary, Coppermill, and Stoneydown.

descriptions: White Fence has territorial beef with KAMSTERS X3.
edges.evidence_quote: engaging in territorial beefs with neighborhoods like Tiny Boys X3 (TBS), KAMSTERS X3 (KAM), Armernian Power X3 (APX3)

descriptions: B40 lists Sindicato do Crime RN as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: GDE , TCP , Nova Okaida, Sindicato do Crime RN, Guardiões do Estado, Bonde do Maluco Bahia, CCA Pará.

descriptions: The Italian Communist Party isolated the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: and were isolated from the Italian political left, including by the Italian Communist Party (PCI)

descriptions: Ait Soussan organisation kidnapped a member of El Ballouti clan due to rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Houssine Ait Soussan allegedly kidnapped him as revenge, because Colombian cartels preferred to work with the El Ballouti clan instead of his organisation.

descriptions: El Ballouti clan and Turtle clan are both involved in bombings aimed at rival gangs, implying cooperation or shared conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Alongside the Mixers clan and Turtle clan, the El Ballouti clan is held responsible for a significant number of bombs and grenades that were thrown in several neighbourhoods of Antwerp, which were aimed at rival gangs.

descriptions: Mixers clan and El Ballouti clan are both involved in bombings aimed at rival gangs, implying cooperation or shared conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Alongside the Mixers clan and Turtle clan, the El Ballouti clan is held responsible for a significant number of bombs and grenades that were thrown in several neighbourhoods of Antwerp, which were aimed at rival gangs.

descriptions: El Ballouti clan is a component of the Moroccan mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The El Ballouti clan is now considered as one of the largest drug trafficking networks in Belgium, and they are also considered to be one of the 5 Moroccan drug clans who control the cocaine trade in the port of Antwerp.

descriptions: Both clans are mentioned together as being exposed by Sky ECC and trafficking cocaine, implying cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The decryption of Sky ECC exposed in particular the inner workings of the Turtle clan and the El Ballouti clan. 27 tonnes of cocaine were also intercepted which were believed to be largely trafficked by these organisations

descriptions: The two sets held a public peace meeting after Nipsey Hussle's death in 2019.
edges.evidence_quote: This started with a public meeting between the Swamp Crips and Campanella Park Pirus at Van Ness Park.

Hermandad de Pistoleros Latinos.description: Mexican American prison gang founded by Chino Avitia in Texas during the early 1980s. It operates in Texas prisons and streets, particularly in Laredo, and is active throughout Mexico with a large contingent in Nuevo Laredo. The gang has over 1,000 members and is involved in drug trafficking, maintaining ties to Mexican drug trafficking organizations.

descriptions: The text states that Mexican drug cartels employ these gangs to distribute drugs, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Among these gangs, that range from street gangs to prison gangs, are the Texas Syndicate, the Latin Kings, the Mexican Mafia, Puro Tango Blast (Vallucos), the Hermandad de Pistoleros Latinos, and the Tri-City Bombers—all based in the Rio Grande Valley and Webb County, Texas.

descriptions: Muto and Scofano-Martello-Ditto-La Rosa are listed together in the 'tela di ragno' operation.
edges.evidence_quote: Le 'ndrine in questione sono: Muto di Cetraro, ... Scofano-Martello-Ditto-La Rosa e Serpa di Paola

descriptions: Scofano-Martello-La Rosa killed the boss of 'Ndrina Serpa, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Francescopio Serpe, boss della famiglia negli anni '90 e nei primi anni '2000, prima di essere ucciso dai rivali Scofano-Martello-La Rosa il 27 maggio 2003

descriptions: Mongrels is a rival of The Americans as it is affiliated with The British.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rivals of the Americans gang include all gangs affiliated to the British gang corporate body. These include the Hard Livings , 28s street gang, Junky Funky Kids, Ghetto Kids, Nice Time Kids, The Terrible West Siders, The Fancy Boys, 6bop gang, Mongrels

Rapid Support Forces.description: Sudanese paramilitary force originating from the Janjaweed militias used in the Darfur conflict. Formally established in August 2013 under Muhammad Dagalo (Hemedti). It fought alongside the Sudanese Armed Forces until 2023, then turned against them in a civil war. The RSF has been accused of war crimes, genocide, and human rights abuses, and has established a parallel government in 2025.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Bara Battalion

descriptions: Members of both families were arrested together, but no relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 18, 2009, the NYPD arrested 22 members and associates of the Luchese and Gambino crime families as part of 'Operation Pure Luck'.

Breed Motorcycle Club.description: The Breed Motorcycle Club was a one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in Asbury Park, New Jersey in 1965 by Salvatore DeIulio. At its peak in the 1970s, it was the most prominent outlaw motorcycle club in New Jersey with chapters in multiple states and 3,000-4,000 members. The club engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder, and had violent conflicts with the Hells Angels and Pagans. It disbanded in 2006 after prominent members were indicted on racketeering and drug trafficking charges.

descriptions: The Breed absorbed Aces and Eights by patching them over in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: The Breed patched over the Riverside , New Jersey–based Aces and Eights Motorcycle Club and the Elizabeth , New Jersey–based Branded Motorcycle Club in 1983

descriptions: The Breed absorbed Branded by patching them over in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: The Breed patched over the Riverside , New Jersey–based Aces and Eights Motorcycle Club and the Elizabeth , New Jersey–based Branded Motorcycle Club in 1983

descriptions: The Breed and Hells Angels fought a deadly brawl in 1971, with ongoing attacks thereafter.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 1971, the club engaged in a confrontation with the Hells Angels in Cleveland , Ohio which resulted in the deaths of five people.

descriptions: Infobox lists Hells Angels as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels, Pagans, Warlocks

descriptions: Breed members joined the Outlaws after the club disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: Several Breed members patched over to the Outlaws and established a chapter in Kensington, Philadelphia.

descriptions: The Breed had a rivalry with the Pagans, involving violence and drug distribution competition.
edges.evidence_quote: The conflict with the Hells Angels, as well as infighting, law enforcement efforts and a rivalry with the Pagans , led to the Breed decreasing in numbers

descriptions: Left-wing journalists claimed the gang had links to P2.
edges.evidence_quote: Some left-wing journalists have also claimed the gang had links to neofascist militant and terrorist groups, such as the Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari (NAR), responsible for the 1980 Bologna massacre; the Italian secret services (SISMI), and political figures such as Licio Gelli, grand-master of the Freemasonic lodge Propaganda Due (P2).

descriptions: Cosa Nostra had ties to the P2 masonic lodge through the Loggia dei Trecento.
edges.evidence_quote: Insieme al cognato Giacomo Vitale (affiliato alla Gran Loggia Madre C.A.M.E A. di Rito Scozzese con sede a Santa Margherita Ligure), Bontate avrebbe fondato la Loggia dei Trecento, molto legata alla P2 di Licio Gelli, cui avrebbero aderito diversi personaggi della Palermo bene.

Clan dei Barcellonesi.description: Mafia clan originating from Barcellona Pozzo di Gotto, Sicily. Active since at least the 1970s, involved in extortion and other criminal activities. The clan's structure has been fluid, with multiple factions and shifting alliances. Its mafia status was not recognized in the 1986 Messina maxi-trial.

descriptions: The Barcellonesi were also allied with the Batanesi faction of the Nebrodi mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: l'ala rimasta "barcellonese" era alleata della fazione dei Santapaola di Cosa Nostra catanese ed anche dei "Batanesi", altra fazione della mafia dei Nebrodi

descriptions: The Barcellonesi faction was allied with the Santapaola faction of Cosa Nostra from Catania.
edges.evidence_quote: l'ala rimasta "barcellonese" era alleata della fazione dei Santapaola di Cosa Nostra catanese

descriptions: The Barcellonesi and Chiofaliani were in a violent conflict, decimating each other.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan barcellonese di Carmelo Milone e quello di Pino Chiofalo si decimarono a vicenda.

descriptions: Rosario Cattafi, a member of the Barcellonesi, was linked to the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: oltre ad essere legato ai Corleonesi di Totò Riina

descriptions: The Barcellonesi have historically been allied with Cosa Nostra from Catania and Palermo.
edges.evidence_quote: Storicamente alleato di Cosa Nostra catanese e palermitana e della mafia dei Nebrodi

descriptions: After the killing of their boss, the Mazzarroti aligned themselves with the Barcellonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: I "mazzarroti" invece con l'uccisione nel 1991 del boss Giuseppe Trifirò, spostarono la loro posizione mafiosa sempre più verso la compagine barcellonese

Folk Nation.description: An alliance of street gangs originating in Chicago, founded in 1978 by Larry Hoover within Stateville Correctional Center. The alliance has spread throughout the United States, particularly the Midwest. Members use symbols like the Star of David and the number 6. It is involved in drug trafficking, burglary, extortion, and homicide.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Black Disciples , Crips

descriptions: Crips and Folk Nation formed an alliance in prisons.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s and early 1990s, as many Crip gang members were being sent to various prisons across the country, an alliance was formed between the Crips and the Folk Nation in Midwest and Southern U.S. prisons.

descriptions: Simon City Royals joined Folk Nation alliance created by Larry Hoover.

Lenox Avenue Gang.description: Early 20th-century New York City street gang based in Harlem, led by Harry Horowitz. Known for muggings, robberies, and murder, it was considered one of the most violent pre-Prohibition gangs. The gang disbanded after key members were executed for the 1912 murder of gambler Herman Rosenthal.
